reason: The passage contains only two named characters—Moses (משה) and Aaron (אהרן)—both of whom are unquestionably elite figures (religious and political leaders of Israel). No regular or common people are named anywhere in the text. The only individuals who have dialogues or direct lines are these two elite figures, who are addressed by both the Pharaoh and God. All other people mentioned—the Egyptians, the children of Israel, sojourners, servants—are referred to collectively or generically (e.g., 'the people,' 'all the congregation of Israel,' 'a sojourner') without individual names or direct speech. This pattern aligns with the statement's assertion that only the elite are named or given dialogue.
